name: node-retry
title: node-retry
summary: Retry strategies for failed operations module for Node.js
description:
Retry strategies for failed operations module for Node.js
node-retry abstracts exponential and custom retry strategies for failed
operations. Its parameters are the number of retries, exponential
factor, minimum and maximum (randomized) timeouts.
.
This also include the node-async-retry module for async context.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side javascript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-retry because Retry strategies for failed operations module for Node.js.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-retry as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-retry with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-retry --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
